text.title: requirements-traceability Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for requirements-traceability as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: 100% forward coverage achieved with one tautological test per requirement.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Coverage metric gaming; pair with AC-density, mutation testing, or independent reviewer sampling. A tautological test is one that passes automatically by design, offering no real validation of the requirement.

failure-mode.description: Horizontal trace blindness — only vertical links (forward/backward) are tracked.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The horizontal axis (derives, conflicts) is where consistency bugs hide and is rarely matrixed by default. Missing horizontal links means derived requirements can diverge silently from parent requirements.

failure-mode.description: Baselining an RTM during volatile discovery.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Formal baselines imply a contract; baselining in flux is theatre and creates change-control overhead for items that should still be under discovery. Baseline only after Approved state.

failure-mode.description: Hand-edited matrix maintained as a spreadsheet instead of generated from source frontmatter.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hand-edited matrices decay within 2-3 sprints; a generator makes the matrix a build product, not a maintenance burden. Every manual update is an opportunity for drift.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated links — agent proposes a link but cannot quote evidence from both artifacts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require verbatim quote from source AND target; auto-reject confidence below 0.9; never accept a link the model proposes but cannot quote.

failure-mode.description: Role conflation — agent confuses satisfies with verifies or derives.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Restate the full five-role vocabulary in the system prompt every turn; do not rely on the model retaining it across turns.

failure-mode.description: Forward bias — agent readily generates BR → FR links and misses TC → REQ (backward) and FR ↔ FR (horizontal).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run three separate passes, one per direction; combine results before human review.

failure-mode.description: Bulk operation runaway — agent edits 500 links in one call with no diff review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cap any agent run at 20 link edits; require human review of the diff before the generator regenerates the matrix.

failure-mode.description: Stale corpus during long agent runs — links point to artifacts that changed after run start.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock the source set with a git SHA at run start; reject the run if the SHA moves before merge.
